# cantonctl

> Institutional-grade CLI toolchain for building on Canton Network

cantonctl provides a Hardhat/Foundry-equivalent developer experience for Daml smart contract development on Canton Network — the enterprise blockchain powering $6T+ in tokenized assets.

## Commands

- `cantonctl init <name>` — Scaffold a project from template (basic, token, defi-amm, api-service, zenith-evm)
- `cantonctl dev` — Start local Canton sandbox with hot-reload, party provisioning, and JWT auth
- `cantonctl build` — Compile Daml contracts via dpm/daml
- `cantonctl test` — Run Daml Script tests with structured output
- `cantonctl deploy <network>` — Deploy DAR to local/devnet/testnet/mainnet
- `cantonctl console` — Interactive REPL connected to Canton node
- `cantonctl status` — Show node health, packages, and parties

## Configuration

Projects use `cantonctl.yaml` (YAML, Zod-validated):
- `project.name`, `project.sdk-version` (required)
- `parties` array with name and role (operator/participant/observer)
- `networks` with type (sandbox/remote/docker), port, url, auth
- `plugins` array of npm packages

## Canton Ledger API V2

Port 7575 (default). Endpoints:
- GET /v2/version
- POST /v2/dars (upload DAR, binary body)
- POST /v2/commands/submit-and-wait
- POST /v2/state/active-contracts
- POST /v2/parties/allocate
- GET /v2/parties

All require JWT Bearer token. For sandbox: HS256 with well-known secret.

## Key Concepts

- **Party**: Named identity (not a hex address). Authorization via signatory/controller.
- **Template**: Daml data type + choices. Like a smart contract class.
- **Choice**: An operation on a contract. Like a contract method.
- **DAR**: Daml Archive. Compiled package uploaded to participant.
- **Signatory**: Party that must authorize contract creation/archival.
- **Controller**: Party that can exercise a choice.

## Error Codes

E1xxx: Configuration, E2xxx: SDK/Tools, E3xxx: Sandbox, E4xxx: Build, E5xxx: Test, E6xxx: Deploy, E7xxx: Ledger API, E8xxx: Console

## Links

- Repository: https://github.com/merged-one/cantonctl
- Canton Network: https://www.canton.network
- Daml SDK: https://docs.daml.com
